session.user:
# /erk:objective-plan
Create an implementation plan for a specific node in an objective's dependency graph.
## Usage
```bash /erk:objective-plan 3679 /erk:objective-plan 3679 --node 2.1 /erk:objective-plan https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/3679 /erk:objective-plan # prompts for objective reference ```
---
## Agent Instructions
### Step 0: Check for Known Node (Fast Path)
Parse `8832` for `--node <node-id>`. If `--node` is present along with an issue number:
**Invoke the inner skill immediately** via the Skill tool:
``` /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <node-id> ```
This skips the interactive selection flow (Steps 1-4 below) since the node is already known. The inner skill handles marker creation, marking as planning, context gathering, plan mode, and saving.
**STOP here** — do not proceed to the steps below.
### Step 1: Resolve Objective Reference
Strip `--node` from `8832` if present, then run:
```bash erk exec resolve-objective-ref 8832 ```
**Interpret the JSON result:**
- If `"resolved": true` with `"source": "branch_name"` or `"source": "plan_metadata"`: Inform user "Using objective #N from {source}. Run with explicit argument to override." - If `"resolved": true` with `"source": "argument"`: Use the `objective_number` directly. - If `"resolved": false`: Use AskUserQuestion to ask "What objective issue should I work from?"
### Step 2: Fetch and Set Up Objective Context
Run:
```bash erk exec objective-plan-setup <objective-number> --session-id "4eb4862b-92c2-4b59-a519-0b92ca4623f7" ```
**Interpret the JSON result:**
- If `"success": false` with `"error": "not_found"`: Report error and exit. - If `"success": false` with `"error": "is_plan"`: Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement`. - If `"success": false` with `"error": "validation_error"`: Report error and exit. - If `"success": true`: Continue with the roadmap data.
Display any `"warnings"` to the user.
### Step 3: Load Objective Skill
Load the `objective` skill for format templates and guidance.
### Step 4: Display Roadmap and Prompt User
Display the roadmap table from the `roadmap.phases` data in the JSON output.
Then use AskUserQuestion to ask which node to plan:
``` Which node should I create a plan for? - Node 1A.1: <description> (Recommended) ← first pending node without plan in progress - Node 1A.2: <description> - Node 2B.1: <description> (plan in progress, #456) ← shown but not recommended - (Other - specify node number or description) ```
**Filtering rules (based on node `status` field from phases):**
- **Show as options:** Nodes with status `"pending"` - **Show but deprioritize:** Nodes with status `"in_progress"` - still selectable via "Other" but not recommended - **Hide from options:** Nodes with status `"done"`, `"blocked"`, or `"skipped"`
**Recommendation rule:** Use `roadmap.next_node` from the JSON as the recommended option. If `next_node` is null, no node is recommended.
If all nodes are complete or have plans in progress, report appropriately:
- All complete: "All roadmap nodes are complete! Consider closing the objective." - All have plans: "All pending nodes have plans in progress. You can still select one via 'Other' to create a parallel plan."
### Step 5: Invoke Inner Skill
After the user selects a node, invoke the inner skill via the Skill tool:
``` /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <selected-node-id> ```
The inner skill handles marker creation, marking as planning, context gathering, plan mode, and saving. **STOP here** — the inner skill takes over.
---
## Output Format
- **Start:** "Loading objective #<number>..." - **After parsing:** Display roadmap nodes with status - **After selection:** "Creating plan for node <step-id>: <description>" - **In plan mode:** Show plan content - **End:** Always proceed with `/erk:plan-save` — do not offer direct implementation
---
## Error Cases
| Scenario | Action | | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | | Objective not found | Report error and exit | | Reference is erk-plan | Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement` | | No pending nodes | Report all nodes complete, suggest closing | | Invalid argument format | Prompt for valid issue number | | Roadmap not parseable | Ask user to specify which node to plan | | Verification fails (no objective_issue) | `/erk:plan-save` handles automatically; follow remediation steps |
---
## Important Notes
- **Objective context matters:** Read the full objective for design decisions and lessons learned - **One node at a time:** Each plan should focus on a single roadmap node - **Link back:** Always reference the parent objective in the plan - **Steelthread pattern:** If planning a Phase A node, focus on minimal vertical slice
## Post-Plan Behavior
**CRITICAL: Objective plans must ALWAYS be saved as PRs.** When the exit-plan-mode hook presents save-or-implement options, ALWAYS proceed with "Create a plan PR" by running `/erk:plan-save`. Do NOT offer to implement directly — objective node plans require PR tracking for the objective's roadmap status to work correctly.
session.tool_result: {"resolved": true, "objective_number": 8832, "source": "argument"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch and set up objective context for #8832

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "objective": {"number": 8832, "title": "Objective: Decouple CLI Commands from Git Repo Requirement", "state": "OPEN", "labels": ["erk-objective"]}, "roadmap": {"phases": [{"number": 1, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 1", "nodes": [{"id": "1.1", "slug": "http-client-always", "description": "Always create HttpClient in context (remove git-repo guard)", "status": "done", "pr": "#8835", "depends_on": []}, {"id": "1.2", "slug": "remote-github-module", "description": "Create RemoteGitHub gateway package (abc/real/fake) with HttpClient-based GitHub operations that take explicit owner/repo params instead of repo_root Path", "status": "done", "pr": "#8835", "depends_on": ["1.1"]}, {"id": "1.3", "slug": "one-shot-repo-flag", "description": "Add --repo flag to one-shot command with remote dispatch path", "status": "done", "pr": "#8835", "depends_on": ["1.2"]}, {"id": "1.4", "slug": "no-repo-infrastructure", "description": "Create @no_repo_required decorator and sentinel gateway infrastructure. Add NoRepoGit/NoRepoGitHub sentinel implementations (following GraphiteDisabled pattern) that raise descriptive errors when repo-dependent operations are called outside a repo context. Wire sentinels into context construction when isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel). The decorator marks Click commands as not requiring a git repo and is inspectable via command.no_repo_required attribute. All commands converted in phases 2 and 3 must use @no_repo_required and route through RemoteGitHub instead of the local subprocess-based GitHub gateway.", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#8880", "depends_on": ["1.3"]}, {"id": "1.5", "slug": "canonical-remote-one-shot", "description": "Make RemoteGitHub the canonical one-shot dispatch path: eliminate the parallel local git dispatch code (one_shot_dispatch.py dispatch_one_shot), unify onto the REST API path (one_shot_remote_dispatch.py), delete the old local dispatch module. Apply @no_repo_required to one-shot command. When --repo is not passed, extract owner/repo from ctx.repo.github and still use RemoteGitHub.", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#8883", "depends_on": ["1.4"]}, {"id": "1.6", "slug": "rename-github-to-local", "description": "Rename GitHub/RealGitHub ABC to LocalGitHub/RealLocalGitHub to clarify it is the subprocess-based local-repo gateway (vs RemoteGitHub REST API gateway). Mechanical bulk rename across ~50 files.", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#8888", "depends_on": ["1.5"]}, {"id": "1.7", "slug": "rename-github-issues-to-local", "description": "Rename GitHubIssues/RealGitHubIssues/FakeGitHubIssues/DryRunGitHubIssues to LocalGitHubIssues/RealLocalGitHubIssues/FakeLocalGitHubIssues/DryRunLocalGitHubIssues. Same mechanical LibCST bulk rename as 1.6, applied to the issues sub-gateway (~142 files).", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["1.6"]}]}, {"number": 2, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 2", "nodes": [{"id": "2.1", "slug": "pr-read-commands", "description": "Convert pr read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: pr list/dash, pr view, pr check, pr log, pr close, pr duplicate-check. Each command routes through RemoteGitHub when --repo is provided or when no local repo is present.", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["1.7"]}, {"id": "2.2", "slug": "objective-read-commands", "description": "Convert objective read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: objective list, objective view, objective check, objective close. Same pattern as 2.1.", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.1"]}, {"id": "2.3", "slug": "discover-repo-refactor", "description": "Refactor discover_repo_context to accept explicit owner/repo as alternative to git discovery, enabling commands to resolve repo identity from --repo flag without a local clone", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.2"]}]}, {"number": 3, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 3", "nodes": [{"id": "3.1", "slug": "pr-create-remote", "description": "Convert pr create to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.3"]}, {"id": "3.2", "slug": "pr-dispatch-remote", "description": "Convert pr dispatch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["3.1"]}, {"id": "3.3", "slug": "launch-remote", "description": "Convert launch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["3.2"]}, {"id": "3.4", "slug": "objective-plan-remote", "description": "Convert objective plan --one-shot to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["3.3"]}]}], "summary": {"total_nodes": 14, "pending": 8, "planning": 0, "done": 3, "in_progress": 3, "blocked": 0, "skipped": 0}, "next_node": {"id": "1.7", "slug": "rename-github-issues-to-local", "description": "Rename GitHubIssues/RealGitHubIssues/FakeGitHubIssues/DryRunGitHubIssues to LocalGitHubIssues/RealLocalGitHubIssues/FakeLocalGitHubIssues/DryRunLocalGitHubIssues. Same mechanical LibCST bulk rename as 1.6, applied to the issues sub-gateway (~142 files).", "phase": "Phase 1", "status": "pending"}, "all_complete": false}, "validation": {"passed": true, "checks": [{"passed": true, "description": "Issue has erk-objective label"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Roadmap parses successfully"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Status/PR consistency"}, {"passed": true, "description": "No orphaned done statuses (done steps have PR references)"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Phase numbering is sequential"}, {"passed": true, "description": "objective-header has objective_comment_id"}, {"passed": true, "description": "PR references use '#' prefix"}]}, "marker_created": true, "warnings": []}
Launching skill: objective
session.user:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/.claude/skills/objective
# Objective Skill
## Overview
Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and capture lessons learned along the way.
**Scope range:**
- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs - Medium: Refactor spanning several plans - Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans
## Objective vs Erk-Plan
| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | | -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | | Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | | Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | | Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown | | Comments | Session context dumps | Action logs + lessons | | Label | `erk-plan` | `erk-objective` | | Tooling | `erk pr dispatch/implement` | Manual updates via comments |
## Key Design Principles
1. **Human-first** - Plain markdown, no machine-generated metadata 2. **Incremental capture** - Each action gets its own comment 3. **Lessons as first-class** - Every action comment includes lessons learned 4. **Clear roadmap** - Status visible at a glance in the body 5. **Body stays current via reconciliation** - After every PR landing, agents audit prose sections against what was actually implemented and correct stale information 6. **Steelthread-first** - Each phase starts with minimal vertical slice proving the concept works 7. **One PR per phase** - Each phase is sized for a coherent single PR 8. **Always shippable** - System remains functional after each merged PR 9. **Body is source of truth** - Body always contains complete current state; comments are the changelog 10. **Two-step for all changes** - Every addition (context, decisions, phases) gets a comment AND body update 11. **Context over code** - Provide references to patterns, not prescriptive implementations 12. **Session handoff ready** - Body should be self-contained for any session to pick up and implement
## Quick Reference
### Creating an Objective
```bash gh issue create --title "Objective: [Title]" --label "erk-objective" --body "$(cat <<'EOF' # Objective: [Title]
> [1-2 sentence summary]
## Goal
[What success looks like - concrete end state]
## Design Decisions
1. **[Decision name]**: [What was decided]
## Roadmap
### Phase 1: [Name] Steelthread (1 PR)
Minimal vertical slice proving the concept works.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 1.1 | [Minimal infrastructure] | pending | | | 1.2 | [Wire into one command] | pending | |
**Test:** [End-to-end acceptance test for steelthread]
### Phase 2: Complete [Name] (1 PR)
Fill out remaining functionality.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 2.1 | [Extend to remaining commands] | pending | | | 2.2 | [Full test coverage] | pending | |
**Test:** [Full acceptance criteria]
EOF )" ```
### Logging an Action
Post an action comment after completing work. See [format.md](references/format.md#action-comment-template) for full template.
```bash gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ## Action: [Brief title] **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD | **PR:** #123 | **Phase/Node:** 1.2 ### What Was Done ### Lessons Learned ### Roadmap Updates EOF )" ```
After posting, update the issue body (roadmap statuses, reconcile stale prose sections).
### Viewing an Objective
View an objective's dependency graph, dependencies, and next node:
```bash # CLI erk objective view <issue-number>
# Slash command (in-session) /local:objective-view <issue-number> ```
### Spawning an Erk-Plan
To implement a specific roadmap node, create an erk-plan that references the objective:
```bash erk pr create --file plan.md --title "Implement [node description]" ```
## Workflow Summary
1. **Create objective** - When starting multi-plan work 2. **Inspect progress** - View dependency graph and next node 3. **Log actions** - After completing each significant piece of work 4. **Update body** - Keep roadmap status current, reconcile stale prose after each PR landing 5. **Spawn erk-plans** - For individual implementation nodes 6. **Close** - When goal achieved or abandoned (proactively ask when all nodes done)
## Resources
### references/
- `format.md` - Complete templates, examples, and update patterns - `workflow.md` - Creating objectives, spawning plans, steelthread structuring - `updating.md` - Quick reference for the two-step update workflow - `closing.md` - Closing triggers and procedures
session.assistant:
Here's the roadmap for objective #8832 — **Decouple CLI Commands from Git Repo Requirement**:
### Phase 1
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 1.1 | Always create HttpClient in context | done | #8835 | | 1.2 | Create RemoteGitHub gateway package | done | #8835 | | 1.3 | Add --repo flag to one-shot command | done | #8835 | | 1.4 | @no_repo_required decorator and sentinel infrastructure | in_progress | #8880 | | 1.5 | Make RemoteGitHub the canonical one-shot dispatch path | in_progress | #8883 | | 1.6 | Rename GitHub ABC to LocalGitHub | in_progress | #8888 | | **1.7** | **Rename GitHubIssues → LocalGitHubIssues (~142 files)** | **pending** | |
### Phase 2
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 2.1 | Convert pr read commands to @no_repo_required | pending | | | 2.2 | Convert objective read commands to @no_repo_required | pending | | | 2.3 | Refactor discover_repo_context for explicit owner/repo | pending | |
### Phase 3
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 3.1 | Convert pr create to @no_repo_required | pending | | | 3.2 | Convert pr dispatch to @no_repo_required | pending | | | 3.3 | Convert launch to @no_repo_required | pending | | | 3.4 | Convert objective plan --one-shot to @no_repo_required | pending | |

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'Which node should I create a plan for?', 'header': 'Node', 'options': [{'la...[truncated 677 chars]...er/repo as alternative to git discovery for --repo flag support'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

The user doesn't want to proceed with this tool use. The tool use was rejected (eg. if it was a file edit, the new_string was NOT written to the file). To tell you how to proceed, the user said: The user wants to clarify these questions. This means they may have additional information, context or questions for you. Take their response into account and then reformulate the questions if appropriate. Start by asking them what they would like to clarify.
Questions asked: - "Which node should I create a plan for?" (No answer provided)
session.assistant: What would you like to clarify about the node selection?
session.user: how many methods are on GithubIssues

session.assistant:
**GitHubIssues has 18 abstract methods:**
1. `create_issue` 2. `issue_exists` 3. `get_issue` 4. `add_comment` 5. `update_issue_body` 6. `list_issues` 7. `get_issue_comments` 8. `get_comment_by_id` 9. `get_issue_comments_with_urls` 10. `ensure_label_exists` 11. `label_exists` 12. `ensure_label_on_issue` 13. `remove_label_from_issue` 14. `close_issue` 15. `get_current_username` 16. `get_prs_referencing_issue` 17. `add_reaction_to_comment` 18. `update_issue_title` 19. `update_comment`
Correction — **19 methods**. That's a sizable ABC, which means node 1.7 will touch implementations in Real, Fake, and DryRun variants plus all call sites across ~142 files.
Would you like to proceed with selecting node 1.7, or did you want to ask about something else before choosing?
session.user: let's leave this rename to later. I want to do a deeper reorg fo the core local github at some point and doing this beforehand would be thrahsy. Lets skip this entirely updates the objective accordingly
session.assistant: I'll update the objective to skip node 1.7. Let me fetch the current issue body first, then update it.

objective-roadmap ```yaml schema_version: '4' nodes: - id: '1.1' slug: http-client-always description: Always create HttpClient in context (remove git-repo guard) status: done pr: '#8835' ... [96 lines omitted] ... pattern) that raise descriptive errors when repo-dependent operations are called
session.assistant: Now I'll update the roadmap to mark 1.7 as skipped, update dependencies for 2.1 to point to 1.6 instead, and post an action comment.

tool_use.param: Save issue body to temp file for editing

[99 lines omitted] ... 46→ pattern) that raise descriptive errors when repo-dependent operations are called

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: gh issue comment 8832 --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ## Action: Skip node 1.7 (rename-github-issues...[truncated 519 chars]...2 nodes (2.1+) can proceed after 1.6 lands; they don't depend on the issues rename. EOF )"
Post action comment explaining why node 1.7 is skipped

session.assistant:
Done. Updated objective #8832: - Node 1.7 marked as **skipped** in the roadmap - Action comment posted explaining the rationale (deeper reorg planned, rename would be throwaway work) - Phase 2 nodes can proceed once 1.6 lands — they don't depend on the issues rename
Want to continue and plan a Phase 2 node, or something else?
session.user: <command-message>objective</command-message> <command-name>/objective</command-name> <command-args>you should have re-rendered the roadmap table when you marked the status as skipp</command-args>

Base directory for this skill: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/.claude/skills/objective
# Objective Skill
## Overview
Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and capture lessons learned along the way.
**Scope range:**
- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs - Medium: Refactor spanning several plans - Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans
## Objective vs Erk-Plan
| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | | -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | | Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | | Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | | Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown | | Comments | Session context dumps | Action logs + lessons | | Label | `erk-plan` | `erk-objective` | | Tooling | `erk pr dispatch/implement` | Manual updates via comments |
## Key Design Principles
1. **Human-first** - Plain markdown, no machine-generated metadata 2. **Incremental capture** - Each action gets its own comment 3. **Lessons as first-class** - Every action comment includes lessons learned 4. **Clear roadmap** - Status visible at a glance in the body 5. **Body stays current via reconciliation** - After every PR landing, agents audit prose sections against what was actually implemented and correct stale information 6. **Steelthread-first** - Each phase starts with minimal vertical slice proving the concept works 7. **One PR per phase** - Each phase is sized for a coherent single PR 8. **Always shippable** - System remains functional after each merged PR 9. **Body is source of truth** - Body always contains complete current state; comments are the changelog 10. **Two-step for all changes** - Every addition (context, decisions, phases) gets a comment AND body update 11. **Context over code** - Provide references to patterns, not prescriptive implementations 12. **Session handoff ready** - Body should be self-contained for any session to pick up and implement
## Quick Reference
### Creating an Objective
```bash gh issue create --title "Objective: [Title]" --label "erk-objective" --body "$(cat <<'EOF' # Objective: [Title]
> [1-2 sentence summary]
## Goal
[What success looks like - concrete end state]
## Design Decisions
1. **[Decision name]**: [What was decided]
## Roadmap
### Phase 1: [Name] Steelthread (1 PR)
Minimal vertical slice proving the concept works.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 1.1 | [Minimal infrastructure] | pending | | | 1.2 | [Wire into one command] | pending | |
**Test:** [End-to-end acceptance test for steelthread]
### Phase 2: Complete [Name] (1 PR)
Fill out remaining functionality.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 2.1 | [Extend to remaining commands] | pending | | | 2.2 | [Full test coverage] | pending | |
**Test:** [Full acceptance criteria]
EOF )" ```
### Logging an Action
Post an action comment after completing work. See [format.md](references/format.md#action-comment-template) for full template.
```bash gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ## Action: [Brief title] **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD | **PR:** #123 | **Phase/Node:** 1.2 ### What Was Done ### Lessons Learned ### Roadmap Updates EOF )" ```
After posting, update the issue body (roadmap statuses, reconcile stale prose sections).
### Viewing an Objective
View an objective's dependency graph, dependencies, and next node:
```bash # CLI erk objective view <issue-number>
# Slash command (in-session) /local:objective-view <issue-number> ```
### Spawning an Erk-Plan
To implement a specific roadmap node, create an erk-plan that references the objective:
```bash erk pr create --file plan.md --title "Implement [node description]" ```
## Workflow Summary
1. **Create objective** - When starting multi-plan work 2. **Inspect progress** - View dependency graph and next node 3. **Log actions** - After completing each significant piece of work 4. **Update body** - Keep roadmap status current, reconcile stale prose after each PR landing 5. **Spawn erk-plans** - For individual implementation nodes 6. **Close** - When goal achieved or abandoned (proactively ask when all nodes done)
## Resources
### references/
- `format.md` - Complete templates, examples, and update patterns - `workflow.md` - Creating objectives, spawning plans, steelthread structuring - `updating.md` - Quick reference for the two-step update workflow - `closing.md` - Closing triggers and procedures
ARGUMENTS: you should have re-rendered the roadmap table when you marked the status as skipp

session.tool_result: Objective # Objective: Decouple CLI Commands from Git Repo Requirement Many erk CLI commands require being in a git repository but only use it to identify the GitHub owner/repo for API calls. By adding an optional `--repo owner/repo` flag, these commands can run from anywhere with just `gh` authentication. ## Goal Any erk command that primarily interacts with the GitHub API can run from any directory when given `--repo owner/repo`. Commands that genuinely need local git state (worktree management, implementation, landing) continue to require a repo. The end state: ```bash # From any directory, no git repo needed: cd /tmp erk one-shot --repo schrockn/erk "fix the typo in README" erk pr list --repo schrockn/erk erk objective list --repo schrockn/erk erk pr dispatch --repo schrockn/erk 123 ``` When inside a git repo, `--repo` is optional — owner/repo is auto-discovered from git remote as today. When outside a git repo, `--repo` is required. Error message when missing: "Not in a git repository. Use --repo owner/repo to specify." ## Design Decisions - **`-R`/`--repo` as optional flag per command** (not global): Only commands that support remote operation get the flag. Matches `gh` CLI convention. When in a git repo without `--repo`, behavior is unchanged. - **HttpClient for remote operations**: Use the existing `HttpClient` (httpx-based) for all GitHub API calls in remote mode, bypassing the `gh api` subprocess calls that require git context for `{owner}/{repo}` template resolution. - **`remote_github/` gateway package**: New 3-file gateway package (`abc.py`, `real.py`, `fake.py`) with HttpClient-based GitHub operations that take explicit `owner`/`repo` params instead of `repo_root: Path`. Shared across all converted commands. - **In-repo behavior unchanged**: When running inside a git repo without `--repo`, all existing code paths are preserved. Zero behavior change for current users. ... [50 lines omitted] ... | 1.4 | Create @no_repo_required decorator and sentinel gateway infrastructure. Add NoRepoGit/NoRepoGitHub sentinel implementations (following GraphiteDisabled pattern) that raise descriptive errors when repo-dependent operations are called outside a repo context. Wire sentinels into context construction when isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel). The decorator marks Click commands as not requiring a git repo and is inspectable via command.no_repo_required attribute. All commands converted in phases 2 and 3 must use @no_repo_required and route through RemoteGitHub instead of the local subprocess-based GitHub gateway. | in-progress | #8880 |
session.assistant: Found it — the prose roadmap table lives in the **objective comment** (comment ID `4011633841`), not the issue body. Let me fetch the full comment, update node 1.7 to "skipped", and push the update.

Same mechanical LibCST bulk rename as 1.6, applied to the issues sub-gateway (~142 files). | pending | - | 49:| 2.1 | Convert pr read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: pr list/dash, pr view, pr check, pr log, pr close, pr duplicate-check. Each command routes through RemoteGitHub when --repo is provided or when no local repo is present. | pending | - | 50:| 2.2 | Convert objective read commands to @no_repo_required with --repo flag: objective list, objective view, objective check, objective close. Same pattern as 2.1. | pending | - | 51:| 2.3 | Refactor discover_repo_context to accept explicit owner/repo as alternative to git discovery, enabling commands to resolve repo identity from --repo flag without a local clone | pending | - | 56:| 3.1 | Convert pr create to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | 57:| 3.2 | Convert pr dispatch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | 58:| 3.3 | Convert launch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | 59:| 3.4 | Convert objective plan --one-shot to @no_repo_required with --repo flag and remote dispatch path via RemoteGitHub | pending | - | 75: status: pending 80: status: pending 85: status: pending 91: status: pending 97: status: pending 103: status: pending 108: status: pending 113: status: pending 118: status: pending 123: status: pending

session.tool_result: 42→| 1.5 | Make RemoteGitHub the canonical one-shot dispatch path: eliminate the parallel local git dispatch code (one_shot_dispatch.py dispatch_one_shot), unify onto the REST API path (one_shot_remote_dispatch.py), delete the old local dispatch module. Apply @no_repo_required to one-shot command. When --repo is not passed, extract owner/repo from ctx.repo.github and still use RemoteGitHub. | in-progress | #8883 | 43→| 1.6 | Rename GitHub/RealGitHub ABC to LocalGitHub/RealLocalGitHub to clarify it is the subprocess-based local-repo gateway (vs RemoteGitHub REST API gateway). Mechanical bulk rename across ~50 files. | in-progress | #8888 | 44→| 1.7 | Rename GitHubIssues/RealGitHubIssues/FakeGitHubIssues/DryRunGitHubIssues to LocalGitHubIssues/RealLocalGitHubIssues/FakeLocalGitHubIssues/DryRunLocalGitHubIssues.
